refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that conflates productivity measurement with operational capability. Refinement separates the measurement/verification aspect from the capability delivery aspect, and explicitly acknowledges dual operator modes (on-site and tele-operated) as distinct execution contexts. This clarification is essential because productivity depends on both machine capability and operator effectiveness, which differ between direct and remote control.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trials under agreed conditions (soil type, task profile, weather, operator experience level). Measure actual throughput (m³/h or ton/h) and compare against productivity_target. Log operator mode (on-site vs. remote), task type, and environmental conditions to establish traceability.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall provide earthmoving capability (bucket capacity, cycle time, reach, force) sufficient to achieve productivity_target under nominal operating conditions.

FR-NGHE-001.2: The Teleoperation system shall enable remote operator to command and monitor the Vehicle with latency and fidelity sufficient to achieve productivity_target equivalent to on-site operation.

FR-NGHE-001.3: The Safety Services shall enforce operational constraints (speed limits, geofence, load limits, emergency stop) without reducing productivity below productivity_target under normal (non-emergency) conditions.

FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE Solution shall provide real-time Telemetry (position, load, cycle state, operator mode) to enable Site Manager to verify productivity_target achievement and diagnose shortfalls.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Maximize machine cycle time efficiency through hydraulic system optimization, bucket geometry, and operator interface responsiveness; measure productivity as throughput per unit time.

DP-B: Implement dual-mode operation (on-site direct control + tele-operated remote control) with mode-specific latency budgets and operator training; measure productivity separately by mode and aggregate.

DP-C: Decompose productivity into machine capability (DP-A) + operator effectiveness (DP-B) + safety constraint enforcement (DP-D); measure each component and validate combined effect on productivity_target.
DP-D: Enforce safety constraints via real-time monitoring and command filtering; design constraint thresholds to permit nominal productivity while preventing unsafe states.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Decompose productivity into machine capability (DP-A: hydraulic/mechanical cycle efficiency) + operator effectiveness (DP-B: dual-mode control with mode-specific latency budgets) + safety constraint enforcement (DP-D: real-time monitoring and command filtering with productivity-preserving thresholds). Measure each component independently and validate combined effect on productivity_target through representative operations trials.

potential_sources: Safety constraint thresholds (DP-D) may couple with cycle efficiency (DP-A) if geofence or load limits are set too conservatively; mitigation: tune thresholds based on trial data.

Teleoperation latency (DP-B) may couple with machine responsiveness (DP-A) if operator cannot compensate for control lag; mitigation: establish latency budget and validate operator performance in trials.
Measurement methodology (Telemetry in DP-C) must be consistent across on-site and remote modes to avoid confounding operator mode with measurement error; mitigation: standardize logging and analysis.
coupling_check.notes: DP-C is a hierarchical decomposition strategy that separates concerns (machine, operator, safety) while acknowledging their interaction. Coupling is manageable through parameterization and empirical validation in trials.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Operator skill and training are held constant (or controlled) across on-site and remote modes; Risk: Untrained remote operators may not achieve productivity_target even if system is capable.

Assumption: Worksite conditions (soil, slope, task profile) are representative of customer's typical operations; Risk: Productivity_target may not be achievable in atypical conditions (e.g., very soft soil, steep slopes).

Assumption: Safety constraints can be tuned to permit nominal productivity without compromising safety; Risk: If safety thresholds are inherently restrictive (e.g., very low speed limits), productivity_target may be unachievable.

Assumption: Telemetry latency and fidelity are sufficient for remote operator to maintain productivity equivalent to on-site; Risk: If latency exceeds operator's cognitive/motor response time, remote productivity will degrade.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-C to explicitly define the latency budget for Teleoperation (DP-B) and the constraint threshold tuning process (DP-D). Add acceptance criteria for each sub-DP (e.g., cycle time ≤ X seconds, latency ≤ Y ms, constraint violations ≤ Z% of operations) to enable independent verification before integration testing.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level integration boundary that orchestrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services to deliver productivity_target. It owns the overall productivity measurement, mode selection (on-site vs. remote), and coordination of safety constraints with operational efficiency.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits real-time telemetry (position, load, cycle state, operator mode, productivity metrics) to Site Manager and Maintenance systems for monitoring and diagnostics.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives task commands (move to location, load, dump, etc.) from Site Manager or Tele-operator, translated into Vehicle control commands and safety-checked before execution.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits safety alerts (constraint violations, emergency stop, geofence breach) to Safety Manager and Operator for situational awareness and incident response.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution uses Network Link to communicate with remote Tele-operator (command/telemetry round-trip) and with Site Manager (monitoring and tasking).

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for productivity orchestration. It owns the integration logic, measurement, and mode coordination. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) cross the boundary.

Vehicle owns machine capability (DP-A: cycle efficiency, hydraulics, bucket geometry) but does not own operator effectiveness (DP-B: teleoperation latency, on-site control responsiveness) or safety constraint enforcement (DP-D: real-time monitoring, command filtering). Productivity measurement and mode coordination must reside at NGHE Solution level, not Vehicle.

Teleoperation owns operator effectiveness in remote mode (DP-B latency budget, remote control interface) but does not own machine capability (DP-A) or safety constraint enforcement (DP-D). Productivity measurement and mode coordination must reside at NGHE Solution level.

Safety Services owns constraint enforcement (DP-D: real-time monitoring, command filtering, threshold tuning) but does not own machine capability (DP-A) or operator effectiveness (DP-B). Productivity measurement and integration with operational efficiency must reside at NGHE Solution level.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary because it owns the integration logic that maps Tasking commands to Vehicle control while enforcing Safety Services constraints and measuring Telemetry against productivity_target. The sub-DPs (DP-A, DP-B, DP-D) are realized by Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services respectively, but their coordination and measurement belong at the NGHE Solution level.
containment_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution becomes a thin orchestration layer without clear ownership of measurement and mode coordination logic, responsibility for productivity shortfalls may be ambiguous. Mitigation: Explicitly allocate measurement logic (cycle time, throughput calculation, mode-specific analysis) to NGHE Solution and document the decision.

Risk: If Safety Services constraint thresholds are tuned independently of NGHE Solution's productivity measurement, safety and productivity may conflict. Mitigation: Establish a joint tuning process where Safety Services and NGHE Solution collaborate on threshold parameterization and validation.

Risk: If Teleoperation latency budget is not enforced at NGHE Solution level, remote operator may experience unpredictable performance. Mitigation: NGHE Solution must monitor Network Link latency and alert Tele-operator if latency exceeds budget.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify the ownership of productivity measurement logic within NGHE Solution: define which module owns cycle time calculation, throughput aggregation, and mode-specific analysis. Establish explicit interfaces between NGHE Solution and Safety Services for constraint threshold tuning and validation. Define latency monitoring and alerting logic within NGHE Solution to ensure Teleoperation latency budget is enforced.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FR (CR-NGHE-001 decomposed into FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4) is directly addressed by the selected DP (DP-C: decomposition into machine capability, operator effectiveness, and safety constraint enforcement). Each sub-FR maps to a corresponding sub-DP, and the combined effect of the sub-DPs is validated through representative operations trials. The DP is logically sound and addresses the high safety criticality by explicitly including safety constraint enforcement (DP-D) as a component of productivity delivery.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion Risk: Productivity measurement logic may be scattered across NGHE Solution, Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services if ownership is not clearly defined. Mitigation: Allocate measurement logic explicitly to NGHE Solution and document the decision.

Diffusion Risk: Safety constraint threshold tuning may be owned by Safety Services without coordination with NGHE Solution's productivity measurement, leading to conflicting optimization. Mitigation: Establish joint tuning process and validation criteria.

Diffusion Risk: Teleoperation latency monitoring may be owned by Teleoperation module without visibility to NGHE Solution, preventing real-time latency-based mode switching or alerting. Mitigation: Allocate latency monitoring to NGHE Solution and define interface to Teleoperation for latency reporting.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary with containment_score 95. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are sub-modules that realize specific sub-DPs but do not own the integration logic, measurement, or mode coordination. The assignment is clear and diffusion risks are manageable through explicit ownership allocation and interface definition.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of CR-NGHE-001 (productivity_target achievement) is feasible through representative operations trials. Measurement of throughput (m³/h or ton/h) is straightforward and can be logged via Telemetry interface. Mode-specific analysis (on-site vs. remote) is feasible if operator mode is tracked in Telemetry. Sub-FR verification (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4) is feasible through component testing (Vehicle cycle time, Teleoperation latency, Safety Services constraint enforcement) and integration testing.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of CR-NGHE-001 to NGHE Solution is clear. The sub-FRs are allocated to Vehicle (FR-NGHE-001.1), Teleoperation (FR-NGHE-001.2), Safety Services (FR-NGHE-001.3), and NGHE Solution (FR-NGHE-001.4). Each allocation is traceable to a specific sub-DP and a specific module. The interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are defined and map to known interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Refine the productivity_target definition to include mode-specific targets (on-site vs. remote) if the customer expects different productivity in each mode. This will clarify acceptance criteria and enable independent verification.

Define acceptance criteria for each sub-FR (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4) in terms of measurable parameters (e.g., cycle time ≤ X seconds, latency ≤ Y ms, constraint violations ≤ Z%). This will enable independent verification before integration testing.

Establish a joint tuning process between Safety Services and NGHE Solution for constraint threshold parameterization. Document the process and validation criteria to ensure safety and productivity are balanced.

Allocate explicit ownership of productivity measurement logic (cycle time calculation, throughput aggregation, mode-specific analysis) to NGHE Solution and document the decision in the design specification.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that has been successfully refined into four atomic sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4), each addressing a distinct aspect of productivity delivery (machine capability, operator effectiveness, safety constraint enforcement, measurement). The selected DP (DP-C: decomposition) is logically sound and addresses the high safety criticality. The assigned IM (NGHE Solution) is appropriate and has high containment_score (95). All three gates (FR-DP, DP-IM, FR-IM) pass. The next step is to decompose each sub-FR to the next level of detail, starting with FR-NGHE-001.1 (Vehicle capability), FR-NGHE-001.2 (Teleoperation effectiveness), FR-NGHE-001.3 (Safety constraint enforcement), and FR-NGHE-001.4 (Telemetry measurement). This will enable detailed design and verification planning.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall provide earthmoving capability (bucket capacity, cycle time, reach, force) sufficient to achieve productivity_target under nominal operating conditions.

FR-NGHE-001.2: The Teleoperation system shall enable remote operator to command and monitor the Vehicle with latency and fidelity sufficient to achieve productivity_target equivalent to on-site operation.

FR-NGHE-001.3: The Safety Services shall enforce operational constraints (speed limits, geofence, load limits, emergency stop) without reducing productivity below productivity_target under normal (non-emergency) conditions.

FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE Solution shall provide real-time Telemetry (position, load, cycle state, operator mode) to enable Site Manager to verify productivity_target achievement and diagnose shortfalls.

assumptions: Operator skill and training are held constant (or controlled) across on-site and remote modes.
Worksite conditions (soil, slope, task profile) are representative of customer's typical operations.
Safety constraints can be tuned to permit nominal productivity without compromising safety.
Telemetry latency and fidelity are sufficient for remote operator to maintain productivity equivalent to on-site.
Productivity_target is achievable with current Vehicle design and Teleoperation technology.
Site Manager and Safety Manager have visibility to Telemetry and SafetyAlert interfaces for monitoring and incident response.
open_risks: Safety criticality (high) may impose constraints that reduce productivity below productivity_target; mitigation: joint tuning process between Safety Services and NGHE Solution.
Teleoperation latency may exceed operator's cognitive/motor response time, degrading remote productivity; mitigation: establish latency budget and validate in trials.
Productivity measurement methodology may differ between on-site and remote modes, confounding results; mitigation: standardize logging and analysis.
Constraint threshold tuning may be iterative and time-consuming; mitigation: establish parameterization process and validation criteria upfront.
evidence_needed: Representative operations trial data: throughput (m³/h or ton/h), cycle time, operator mode, environmental conditions, constraint violations.
Vehicle cycle time specification and validation data (hydraulic system performance, bucket geometry, operator interface responsiveness).
Teleoperation latency measurement and operator performance data (remote vs. on-site productivity comparison).
Safety Services constraint threshold tuning data and validation results (constraint violations, safety incidents, productivity impact).
Telemetry logging and analysis methodology documentation (measurement definitions, aggregation logic, mode-specific analysis).
